BMR homes are specified units inSanFranciscosold at belowmarketrate prices. They are also resold at belowmarketrate prices to future eligible buyers. Data Views The dashboard below features an interactive map of San Francisco with four major data views (buttons) located along the left side of the dashboard. These clickable view buttons are: *   Pipeline *   Completed (Units) *   Major Multiphase (Projects) *   Authorized (Units) \u2013 San Francisco\u2019s primary metric for tracking Regional Housing Needs Allocation progress When a button is selected, the total for that view is indicated in the upper left corner (under the title Housing Dashboard) with a breakdown of related subtotals shown to the right and along the top of the dashboard (e.g., under review, new construction, complete, etc). While the Pipeline and Completed data views are mutually exclusive (a unit is either completed or in the pipeline), the Authorized and Major Multiphase views offer alternative ways to explore the same data (projects in these views are also counted in either the Pipeline or Completed Units views, depending on their status). Data results can also be switched between a Map View or Table View by clicking on either button title, located on the right side of the dashboard along with these additional filters: *   Analysis Neighborhoods *   Supervisor Districts, and *   Planning Areas\n...\nTitle: San Francisco Housing Dashboard | SF Planning URL Source: https://sfplanning.org/san-francisco-housing-dashboard Markdown Content: The Housing Dashboard offers a comprehensive look at housing production in San Francisco, showcasing completed dwelling units since 2005 and tracking active residential projects in the development pipeline. Data Views The dashboard below features an interactive map of San Francisco with four major data views (buttons) located along the left side of the dashboard. These clickable view buttons are: *   Pipeline *   Completed (Units) *   Major Multiphase (Projects) *   Authorized (Units) \u2013 San Francisco\u2019s primary metric for tracking Regional Housing Needs Allocation progress When a button is selected, the total for that view is indicated in the upper left corner (under the title Housing Dashboard) with a breakdown of related subtotals shown to the right and along the top of the dashboard (e.g., under review, new construction, complete, etc). While the Pipeline and Completed data views are mutually exclusive (a unit is either completed or in the pipeline), the Authorized and Major Multiphase views offer alternative ways to explore the same data (projects in these views are also counted in either the Pipeline or Completed Units views, depending on their status).
